Name: The Perks of Being a Wallflower
Review:
Enjoyed it at times but tbh it was defo not my type. very predictable and soul-less at times. But I did find a good quote from this one-
we accept the love we think we deserve.
Maybe it has something to do with this genre. I hate this feeling, the one I had while watching it. This movie does made me realise a thing or two about myself and it got me thinking about it for a couple of hours. So as an ultimate act of bravery and hope that no one will read this I will put this in public so please do not, yes do not read what's below. Bye. Leave. Thx. So you've decide to stick so if you know me and see me irl never ever mention this, I'll go a lil bit off topic here, I felt something i don't actually feel. Maybe that's why I felt bad, honestly idk. Again apologies for over-sharing so bear with me, I had no friends in hs and barely anyone to talk to in my college but I still believe im not doing pretty fine because I have myself (copium) i know it gets pretty isolating at times but guess what, you've gotta deal with it. I wish I had a super cool friend but safe to say I feel like I am the problem, there truly is no one to whom I can open up, or share how's my day going, how's my life going, how's the counter strike major going, i truly feel alone but all I gotta do it's cope with it. I hope someday it'll get better. Untill next time, seeya :)

Name: Wake Up Dead Man
Review: I'm pretty sure I will never forget this movie. From the narrative to storytelling to lighting and cinematography, everything was super well cut out and makes me feel so happy watching it for the first time. When it ended, it had me like wow, what did I just watch, and trust me, I said it not in a bad way. Lets start with the cinematography part, the use of the sun as a lighting source is genuinely stunning. Three scenes are etched in my mind: First, the forest confession scene. Father Jude becomes so consumed with solving the mystery and with material concerns that he loses sight of his true purpose as a priest. The phone call with Louise pulls him back onto the path of bringing people together. The scene itself with the tonal shift was soo powerful. It has to be my favorite scene in the entire franchise!!! Second, the scene where the detective stops the father from doing the false confession by playing the organ. It is such a small moment, but incredibly cool. this right here Third, scene where the warmth and light slowly drain from the space as Blanc explains why he is not a believer. Watching the glow fade while faith is questioned was just "absolute cinema". oof i can keep writing all night long. But yeah soo much to say, too little time in our hands. Holy smoke, this movie was a banger. Daniel Craig is such a g. Also, the detail that there are two different shots depending on which version of the story Martha is telling is incredible. Rian Johnson you fucin' genius. And the way sunlight subtly changes during interior scenes was soo clever like the moment u notice it just feels soo surreal. There is so much more to talk about, but yeah. This was such a good watch. Oh my god im flabbergasted. Btw writing this review during map break of StarLadder Budapest Major. I just hope VITA win guys edit: vita won 3-1 less goo bby stay safe!

Name: Notting Hill
Review: I do not believe in such a madeup concept as of love, and I just simply dont believe it exists. But this movie made me question my belief. How can two people with two completely different worlds meet under such fanciful circumstances? Notting Hill is pure art. It's beauty in the struggle, ugliness in the success. Possibly, I will add it to my comfort movie list, because I kid you not, it felt very, very soothing watching this. I was really procrastinating on watching this movie for so long prolly over a month, but today I decided why not let’s watch this bright and early in this lovely winter morning. Well, I did just that, and now I consider it a big mistake, because I cannot think of anything else. That's what this movie has been for me. Also here is a absolute kino dialogue from this movie atleast: William: I live in Notting Hill. You live in Beverly Hills. Everyone in the world knows who you are, my mother has trouble remembering my name. Anna: I'm also just a girl, standing in front of a boy, asking him to love her. And when spike says, I knew a girl at school called Pandora. Never got to see her box, though. Oh boyy Side note: this movie has a When Harry Met Sally reference about Meg Ryan having an orgasm whenever you took her to coffee. It quietly reminded me of Mr. Reiner's masterpiece now that he is no longer with us I became sad for a while. But that is what life is.. Thank you Mr.Reiner for your works. The Beautiful Simplicity of Love in Notting Hill! That is super hard to capture, and they really did a great job doing so. I really loved it, per se.

Name: Sleepless in Seattle
Review:
I love you Nora Ephron, and yes i mean it,

I love you for giving me two of the purest romcoms ever made: Sleepless in Seattle and You’ve Got Mail. 

What I figured out was love is not a truth nor a promise, its something that happens when people hesitate long enough, to notice each other.

Also Love is not destiny, but as a momentary alignment, accidental, and real only while it lasts.

I love you. Regard
me

Name: Adventures in Babysitting
Review: I really wanted to write this for a long time, so here goes nothing: Adventures in Babysitting 1987 directed by Chris Colombus is by far one of my favorite movies, It presents chaos as a shortcut to maturity. It gives me a solid glimpse of one single night filled with danger and improv, yet optimism! somehow it feels oddly comforting. Allowing fear to exist without ethical weight is such an interesting concept used here, and is partly the reason the film make me feel this way. It also captures the emotional truth of growing up and not being 17 anymore as a feeling of being hurled forward by forces we cannot control, such is life. While quietly avoiding the more unsettling reality that growth is rarely bestowed by chaos itself. Now time to talk about my favorite character, Chris! I loved the character arc of Chris. She does not begin the night as a hero. But she becomes hyper-competent under pressure. As the night worsens, she grows decisive, fearless, and morally centered. As events unfold, Chris’s emotional center shifts. By the end, she stops measuring herself through romantic approval and begins acting from internal conviction. We can clearly observe, this marks a movement from external validation to a much more grounded agency. She realizes that love is no longer something that defines her worth. She returns home unchanged on the surface. The same house, the same job, the same social position. But the night has quietly reorganized her inner hierarchy of values. Her boyfriend’s rejection no longer defines her emotional center. What mattered was not being chosen, but choosing correctly under pressure. The film affirms that dignity can emerge without romantic validation. Chris carries something others cannot see: proof that she can act decisively, protect others, and remain humane inside fear. Chicago is portrayed as overwhelming but not nihilistic, a city that rewards awareness rather than innocence which makes it much more human, philosophically. For me, the film is about trust in momentum. There is no ethical discomfort. It treats the city as an external test rather than a human-made structure. The film understands night- time as a liminal space where ordinary rules dissolve and identity becomes fluid. The ending restores everyone's normal life without forgetting the experiences they have had. The night matters. Even if it leaves no visible scars. This strongly affirms that not all meaningful experiences need permanent markers. :) Some truths are internal and carried forward quietly. That's enough Mr.West now go back to your stupid chungus life! :) also i used a bit of ai writing this to correct a few oopsie so dont mind me being as cs major. regards, me ofc!

Name: Sleepless in Seattle
Review:
Jumped straight to this after watching Sentimental Values. just wanna thank Nora Ephron for making a piece art that I hold soo dear to my heart and i can truely say that its my safe space. :)

And adding to that, Rob Riener had such a heart touching cameo in this one, i just can't be grateful enough for this movie.

Plus, one dialogue that really stuck with me was this right here:

Doctor Yapper: Sam, tell me what was so special about your wife.

Sam: Well, how long is your program? Oh, well, it was a million tiny little things that, when you added them all up, they... they just meant we were supposed to be together. And I knew it, I knew it the very first time I touched her. It was like coming home, only to no home I'd ever known. I was just taking her hand to help her out of a car. And I knew it. It was like... magic.

Absolute Cinema. ♥️

Name: Marty Supreme
Review:
an absolute shit-show of a movie. The only possibly praiseworthy part of this movie was the initial 30 minutes, because the rest of the movie was just unwatchable,boring selfcontradicting bullshit.

Let's talk about the story first. The story was all over place. Don't get me wrong, but looking back, why would it be this way? The side characters do had a lot of potential but guess what? The subplot is not even noteworthy. Whole bunch of dog chasing and spanking (literally). Now the ENDING. There were essentially no stakes in the ending. What did he achieve? Winning in an exhibition match??? Proving to a crowd that the self proclaimed greatest of ping pong won against a nonchalant Japanese undergod after getting whopped on the world stage? What even is the point of this movie? The ending felt artificial. I was hyped up for nothing, glad I didn't go to the theater to watch this. If adding good songs in the opening and closing creds would've made a movie great it would've already been the greatest fucking movie of all time.

I kid u guys not, I wasted 2½ hours of valentine's afternoon on this? Nah man I feel robbed even pirating this.

A piece of overhyped, overmarketed trash.

Name: Petite Maman
Review:
Oh my heavens, I think this is it. This is the movie I’ve been looking for all my life. A perfect depiction of incurable sadness, guilt, and coping.

So now it’s time for a long review, because I don’t bother writing one unless i have that gut feeling that it will truly be worth it. So, here goes nothing:

To start off, it’s a beautiful film. Again great  emphasis on the word beautiful. Everything about it feels magical, from the direction and setting to the atmosphere and lighting. I can’t really put into words exactly how I felt after watching it because it’s simply overwhelming, especially the scene where the ending revelation happens and how they both act toward each other.

The whole plot could just be a dream, but even if it is, it’s the most beautiful dream she will ever have. It’s such a simple story on the surface, but underneath it carries this deep, almost unspoken understanding of grief, memory, and childhood.

I don’t think it tries to overwhelm me, but somehow it still did. I was holding my emotions back. I just wish it lasted longer. Céline Sciamma is so, so good at this kind of tender, soft storytelling with that quiet, meditative pacing. It doesn’t feel like a typical magical story at all, more like a memory or a dream you’re slowly remembering. A kind of fairytale where even the most fantastical parts feel familiar, like small, sweet quirks of childhood.

I don’t think I’ve ever smiled this much while watching a film. It feels like a warm hug hidden inside a movie.

And that line, “you didn’t invent my sadness”, like how could you even come up with something sooo deep like this. Its like a dagger through my fucking heart. 

This shit straight up melted my heart, but not in a simple way, made me question what it really means to say sorry, goodbye, and “it’s okay.” ???
Céline made tenderness feel precise, where comfort exists alongside something unresolved and what a perfect execution by the cast. Hats Off!

Honestly, big fan of whatever the french are doing with cinema.

- love, shivansh

Name: Punch-Drunk Love
Review:
A beautiful film. The way it captures human emotion is genuinely astonishing.

Watching Adam Sandler cry is such a humbling experience. 

Talking about the experience, isn’t this movie a goddamn fever dream? 

Like everything feels like it’s on the verge of exploding and the world is about to collapse, and then it doesn’t. Anddd, then you get that same feeling again, and still nothing happens. 

And can i just appreciate the cool as fuck fight sequence, it was sooo fucking gas dude, even the background score went crazyy! banger

Absolutely loved this one. Great job, Mr. PTA :)

Name: Rosetta
Review: The moment Rosetta decides to off herself, the cylinder runs empty mid suicide, as if her poverty is laughing at her. Not letting her live as well as not letting her die peacefully. The image of her hauling a gas bottle through the trailer park with Riquet circling on his bike is the whole film compressed into one shot. Then finally she just collapses and Riquet, the same guy she sold out, the only person who was ever decent to her, kills his engine and helps her up. funny thing Dardennes don't explain it. Maybe she lets him in. Maybe tomorrow she finds another tank. The bit where she lies in bed talking to herself, just repeating that she's normal, that she has a job, that everything is fine, is the moment this film became a masterpiece,, here she isnt convincing anyone. she is running a script she wrote for herself because there's nobody else to run it for her. The film ends on her face, first time she's looked at another person like they're a person and not a threat, and leaves to figure out if that's supposed to be hope or just a pause. I love how its filmed on a handheld, strapped to his shoulder so we can see whats its like to be in her head, the effective framing gives the first hand experience and feel. Such a beautiful work Read More HERE!!

Name: Incendies
Review: Made me cry, vomit, feel guilt shame sadness anger disgust empathy all in the same moment. This is what cinema is meant to be. Masterpiece. I would much happily kill myself than ever watching this again. It's been hours but I still feel nauseous. I can never feel the emotion I felt before watching this. I feel like a completely new and terrible version of myself now while writing this. Truly a moving piece of media. after magnolia and aftersun, this will be the movie i will always think of when I'm going to sleep. i can never be the same person i was before, yet I shall continue to exsist. marhaban

Name: Dawn of the Dead
Review: what a great movie, really makes u ponder who are the real villains, the humans or the zombies, such an amazing theme of how people respond when the structures that give life meaning disappear and systems corrode. and how comfort can become a prison as the mall itself starts as a sanctuary. Overtime, it becomes something closer to a cage. The characters become attached to comfort and routine making them reluctant to move on even when staying longer means certain death like the cellar in the first movie.

Name: Day of the Dead
Review:
Amazing movie. Bub is hands down the greatest zombie OAT and with Rhodes being easily the most unlikable character in the entire franchise, it was incredibly satisfying to see him get what was coming to him.

The bg score was fantastic, mixed with da the constant sense of paranoia mixed with dread, nothing short of the lord's work. Genuinely impressive work.
